meaning: adj.骄傲的,自大的,过于自信的
examples: They got very cocky, and went about saying you were done for this time!
He was a little bit cocky when he was about 11 because he was winning everything.
"Yes, he is rather cocky," Wendy admitted with regret.
To start with, back in October, he had been confident, even a little cocky.
And you can be certain they came into another game cocky, complacent, and unprepared.
